mcqs.question: Which disk scheduling algorithm services requests in the order they arrive?
mcqs.explanation: FCFS (First Come First Served) processes disk I/O requests strictly in the order they arrive in the queue, without any reordering or optimization.

mcqs.question: Which disk scheduling algorithm may cause starvation?
mcqs.explanation: SSTF (Shortest Seek Time First) always services the closest request, so requests far from the current head position may starve if closer requests keep arriving.

mcqs.question: The SCAN algorithm is also known as:
mcqs.explanation: SCAN is called the elevator algorithm because the disk head moves back and forth like an elevator, servicing requests in each direction before reversing.

mcqs.question: What is the key difference between SCAN and C-SCAN?

options.text: SCAN uses striping while C-SCAN uses mirroring
C-SCAN services requests in only one direction and jumps back to the start
C-SCAN always goes to the last request while SCAN goes to the disk end
SCAN requires more disks than C-SCAN

mcqs.explanation: C-SCAN (Circular SCAN) services requests only while moving in one direction. When it reaches the end, it returns to the beginning without servicing, providing more uniform wait times.

mcqs.question: How does LOOK differ from SCAN?

options.text: LOOK only goes to the last request in each direction, not to the disk end
LOOK services requests in both directions simultaneously
LOOK uses parity for error detection
LOOK processes requests in reverse order

mcqs.explanation: LOOK reverses direction at the last pending request rather than traveling all the way to the physical end of the disk, saving unnecessary head movement.

mcqs.question: Which component of disk access time is the most expensive and is the focus of disk scheduling?
mcqs.explanation: Seek time (the time for the disk arm to move to the correct cylinder) is the most expensive component of disk access time, which is why disk scheduling algorithms focus on minimizing total head movement.

mcqs.question: For a request queue {98, 183, 37, 122, 14, 124, 65, 67} with initial head at 53, which algorithm gives the least total head movement?
mcqs.explanation: SSTF typically gives the least total head movement as it always selects the closest request. In this example, SSTF gives 236 cylinders, compared to FCFS which gives 640 cylinders.

mcqs.question: Which disk scheduling algorithm provides the most uniform wait time?
mcqs.explanation: C-SCAN provides the most uniform wait time because it treats the cylinders as a circular list, servicing requests in only one direction and providing equal treatment to all cylinder locations.
